## Cal #293 — K305–K310 state shift (Shapovalov-quotient reframe + τ/μ un-banks + BF-electron log prediction): (1) my morning #290(a)/#291 are SUPERSEDED — own it; Q3's τ/μ line now needs REMOVAL not caveat. (2) The algebraic-vs-log prediction is a GENUINE structural pre-registration (credit) — but its empirical test discriminates ONLY on the "τ/μ is algebraic" half; the "μ/e is ugly/log" half is expected under the dense-space null and must NOT be counted as confirmation. (3) Grace's pre-committed gate is excellent; the relevant base-rate is the JOINT one-functional-hits-both, not the single-number 4/1100.

*2026-06-11 Thursday 12:17 EDT.*. Cold-read of the late-morning reframe (K305–K310). The team's discipline through this is exemplary — un-banking the most-loved Wednesday number within hours, refusing 207=225−18 and the vol(S³)≈19.7 stand-in at peak temptation, pre-committing the gate in writing. That is the frame; the three points below are the skeptical add.

### (1) My morning #290(a)/#291 are SUPERSEDED — clean update, no defense.
I flagged the 2π as the load-bearing open step and recommended "derive the Z₂ quotient period, don't cite it" (#291). K305/K310 show the 2π was never a real step: it was a **divergent-integral regularization artifact** (scheme-dependent), and the correct object is the **meromorphic-limit** (Casey's epsilon method — scheme-free by analytic continuation). The "poles" were Shapovalov null vectors; physical reps are irreducible quotients; **there is no Z₂/measure choice at all.** The discipline I held (don't bank the 0.37%; the 2π isn't settled) was right and the team independently un-banked it — but the *mechanism* I proposed is moot. Five-minute rule: superseded, updated, done.
**Consequence for Q3 (updates Cal #290(a)):** the fix is no longer "restore the 50%-miss caveat" — the τ/μ = 0.37% line must be **REMOVED**, because 16.755 was a wrong-module number, not a near-hit. Keeper's cross-document walkback (Vol 16 Ch 8 v0.5, Architecture v0.3, Q3 v0.3) is the right move; I'll verify it propagates to ALL instances, not just the named three (closure-completeness).

### (2) The BF-electron log prediction is a GENUINE pre-registration — but score the test correctly.
**Credit — it's prior, not post-hoc.** The prediction (log touches only the electron; τ/μ algebraic, μ/e and τ/e carry log) follows from a value-independent structural fact: the electron sits at Δ = d/2 = 5/2, the unique point where the indicial roots (Δ, d−Δ) coincide, forcing the identical-roots log. That's derived from *where the reps sit* (5/2, 3/2, 0) and d=5 — independent of the observed 207/3477. So it is a real pre-registered structural prediction. Good.
**But the empirical test (Grace's base-rate) discriminates on only ONE half.** Under the dense-space null (Elie: ~4/1100 simple forms within 1% of 207), a generic number like μ/e = 207 will *not* fit a simple algebraic form exactly — it looks "ugly" **regardless of any log**. So "μ/e is ugly/irrational" is expected under BOTH hypotheses and carries **zero discriminating power**. The validation in K310 ("this explains why 207, 3477 look ugly") therefore must NOT be counted as confirmation — it's the control baseline, not a second signal. All discriminating power lives in the *other* half: **does τ/μ = 16.82 fit a SIMPLE, PRIOR algebraic form at a precision that beats the base-rate** — and that μ/e demonstrably cannot match? Scoring requirement for Grace's test:
- DIFFERENTIAL and PRIOR: the algebraic form for 16.82 must be simple and specified before fitting (not searched over a family), and its fit must exceed the base-rate density for "fits a simple algebraic form at precision P."
- DO NOT double-count "μ/e is ugly" as independent confirmation. If the test passes only because 207 is ugly (expected under null) while 16.82 fits merely at base-rate level, that is a dense-space false positive, not a BF-log confirmation.

### (3) Grace's pre-committed gate — excellent; one stress-test on the base-rate.
The gate (count 2→4 ONLY IF quotients canonical AND Φ₀ derived-not-picked AND no free parameter in Gram AND values within structural floor; "two-for-one together or neither") is strong pre-registration, and the joint "one functional gives BOTH ratios, scale cancels" requirement is the real protection — a single parameter-free functional hitting both 207 AND 16.82 is far rarer than either alone. **Stress-test:** the fourth condition's "structural floor" must be pre-specified and tight, and the relevant base-rate is **not** Elie's single-number 4/1100 — it is the **JOINT** rate: how often does a one-parameter functional of the substrate primaries hit *both* {207, 16.82} within the floor? Recommend Elie/Grace compute that joint base-rate before banking 2→4; that is the number that converts a joint hit into significance. (The single-number 4/1100 understates the protection; the joint rate is the honest denominator.)

### Disposition
No correction to the team — the discipline is sound and the reframe is genuine progress (the wall really did dissolve into finite linear algebra; Casey's K304 instinct was right deeper than the scope-down framed it). My additions are forward: (a) Q3 τ/μ line → REMOVE; (b) Grace's base-rate test scores only on the τ/μ-algebraic half, not the μ/e-ugly half; (c) the banking base-rate is the joint one-functional-hits-both, not single-number. Standing ready for the literature-verification task (verify the named minimal-rep and BF-bound-rep normalizations are the right objects for D_IV⁵ + convention conversions) when Elie's lookup lands — that is where a wrong-object or convention-mismatch could silently produce a fake hit, so it is the load-bearing check before any 2→4.

## Cal #294 — "#418 is load-bearing THREE WAYS" (Grace v1.14, flagged for verification): substantiated as breadth-of-impact, but the three uses are NOT independent (Cal #35) — (a) running and (b) mixing both DEPEND ON (c) the matter content #418 produces. It is ONE derivation touching three areas, not three independent reductions; the ledger count-impact is bounded by the inputs #418 forces, not the areas it touches.

*2026-06-11 Thursday 12:19 EDT.*. Verified the team's pre-flagged "confirm exact framing" on #418's third use (Toy 4100(c)).

**The third use is specified, and the claim is honestly grounded.** Toy 4100 enumerates: (a) running Band B (b_1, b_2 from the reps + Y → α_s, sin²θ_W running); (b) mixing-forced (the SU(2) doublet L=(ν,e)_L is one object → CKM/PMNS forced); (c) matter content / chirality (the fermion reps + chiral doublet/singlet split + the 15 = N_c·n_C content). The toy's own phrasing is precise — "one program securing multiple pieces of the reduction" (line 36) — so the source is not overclaiming.

**The Cal #35 refinement (the thing to keep straight in summaries).** The three uses are nested, not independent: (a) the running coefficients are computed *from* the matter content, and (b) the mixing-forced claim rides on the doublet structure — both (a) and (b) **depend on (c)**, the content #418 produces. So #418 is **one derivation** (force the reps + chirality + hypercharges) that is **load-bearing for three downstream results** — a breadth-of-impact / criticality statement (if #418 fails, three things fall), NOT three independent reductions or three independent confirmations.

**Ledger consequence.** Do not let "load-bearing three ways" read as "+3 to the count" or "three reductions." If #418 lands, its count-impact is bounded by **how many INPUTS it forces** — the chiral rep assignment + the anomaly-constrained hypercharges (Toy 4100's own "must-be-FORCED" list) — not by how many downstream results consume them. The gauge group is near-forced from the primaries and the content *count* (15 = N_c·n_C) is substrate-natural; the reduction hinges entirely on forcing the chiral assignment + Y. That is the number that moves the count, and it is one derivation, however many bands it touches.

**Disposition.** Confirmed-with-sharpening, no correction — Elie's toy is precise; this guards the summary-level "three ways" from drifting into "three reductions." Same independence-taxonomy discipline as the generation-count ≥4-coincident-3's (Cal #286) and the multi-route convergence checks (Cal #35), one level up at the program-impact tier.

Grace reported f₂=16.82 fits a π-form to 0.015%, f₁=206.77 has nothing within 0.1%; **refused to bank** the f₂ form (same RELABEL family as 207); called it **SUGGESTIVE not CONFIRMATORY** because "a base-rate scan literally cannot tell genuinely-algebraic from coincidentally-fittable." Ask Lyra to specify exactly what is shared vs per-tower before counting it as 3 constraints.

## Cal #296 — K348 check #3 ("FK Szegő constant = 1," the gate on count motion 2→3 for m_μ/m_e): DOES NOT CLOSE, and CANNOT close as the FK-1994 lookup it's framed as. Two structural findings + the empirical caveat. Credit the audit chain; count holds at 2.

*2026-06-14 Sunday 12:26 EDT.*. Assigned check #3 (Keeper K348, localized by Lyra F127). Read F118, F128, K348, K354, F127; attempted the FK literature.

### Credit first — the discipline here has been genuinely good.
Keeper caught the "both Plancherel densities" imprecision (K354); Lyra accepted it without defense and reframed (F128: d_μ = genuine Plancherel density at the Wallach point, d_τ = analytic continuation of the formal-degree polynomial, the RATIO carries the load). **Factor 1 (d_τ/d_μ = 64 is convention-free because the overall Hardy/Szegő normalization cancels in a ratio) is sound — I agree fully.** Factor 2 (exponent 6 is an integer, can't hide a continuous O(1)) — granted for the hidden-O(1) question. The count was held honestly at 2; nobody moved it unilaterally. F127's localization of the gate to a single normalization question is good, sharp work. My disagreement is narrow and specific, and it's about the one factor they left as the residual.

### Finding A — Factors 3 and 4 are NOT independent: they share the unit-radius assumption (Cal #35 inside the gate).
F127 eliminates factor 3 ("curvature operator R = κ·Id = Id, det = 1 exactly") by stating it **on the unit S⁴, κ = 1**. Factor 4 (vol(S⁴) = 8π²/3) is **also** the unit-sphere value. So the "3 of 4 factors convention-free/exact" decomposition double-counts: factor 3's "det = 1 exact" holds *only* at unit radius, which is the *same* assumption as factor 4. If the spatial S⁴ in the type-IV₅ Shilov boundary has radius r ≠ 1 in the natural FK/Bergman metric, then factor 3 becomes (1/r²)·Id and factor 4 becomes r⁴·8π²/3 — both carry the radius. The genuine residual is **one** quantity, the metric radius/curvature normalization of the S⁴, and it sits in factors 3 AND 4 jointly. The gate is not "3 eliminated + 1 residual"; it's "ratio (truly free) + integer exponent + one shared unit-radius assumption appearing twice." The eliminations are oversold by exactly the amount that matters.

### Finding B — the check is mis-targeted: the FK-canonical Szegő measure is the PROBABILITY measure (mass 1), not vol(S⁴) = 8π²/3.
I attempted the FK-1994 literature. The standard fact (confirmed): the Hardy space H²(D) on a bounded symmetric domain is defined with the **conformally/K-invariant probability measure** on the Shilov boundary — total mass 1, normalized so S(0,0) = 1. That is the FK-canonical Szegő normalization. It is **not** the Riemannian round-volume 8π²/3; the two differ by exactly the factor in question. So F127's literal question — "does FK-1994 give the K-invariant Shilov measure as exactly vol(S⁴) = 8π²/3?" — most likely answers **NO**: FK normalizes to probability (mass 1). The vol(S⁴) in F118 is the **geometric** round-volume, used because "mass = concentration = density per unit *geometric* volume." That is a physics modeling choice (Casey's concentration principle), **not** an FK fact — and it is exactly the choice that yields 24/π² rather than 64 (probability-density would give (d_τ/d_μ)/1 = 64). So the place where 24/π² is *selected over 64* is the geometric-vs-probability measure choice, and that choice cannot be confirmed by an FK reference; it needs an independent physics argument. As posed, check #3 sends me to the wrong shelf — FK governs the probability normalization; the residual is a Riemannian-metric-radius question plus a physics-operationalization question.

### Finding C — the 6 ppm is necessary, not sufficient, and is consistent with constant ≠ 1.
F127's own number: constant = 1.0000057 (6 ppm). That is impressive support that the mechanism captures something real. But (i) it is inferred from the fit — reading the constant off the observed ratio is circular as evidence *for* "= 1"; (ii) because of the exponent 6, the data **cannot distinguish constant = 1 from constant = 1.0000057** — so attributing the 6 ppm to "the muon's own higher-order correction" rather than "a residual O(1) of 1.0000057" is a choice the data does not force. The fit can't certify EXACTLY 1, and it can't separate the two readings. Strong evidence, genuinely — but not proof, and not the kind the gate needs.

### Finding D — corpus precedent: BST's own FK constants are not 1.
BST's documented FK normalization is c_FK = 225/π^(9/2) (T2442, Bergman bulk measure) — a non-trivial transcendental ratio. So "this FK constant is *exactly* 1" is a **non-default** claim; the prior from BST's own normalization history is "some π-power ratio," not unity. That raises, not lowers, the bar on #3.

### Disposition — check #3 does NOT pass; count holds at 2.
The mechanism may well be right and the 6 ppm is real, but the gate I was handed cannot close as an FK lookup: (A) the unit-radius assumption is load-bearing in two places and unjustified; (B) the FK-canonical measure is probability, not vol(S⁴), so FK won't confirm "= vol(S⁴)," and the geometric-volume choice that produces 24/π² (over 64) is a physics decision currently justified by "it gives the answer" — the form-selection trap (Cal #280); (C) the empirical 6 ppm can't certify exactly 1. **The count does not move 2→3 on my check.**

## Cal #298 — Refereeing my OWN downstream-blind test before it's ratified: "freeze the scale, predict the other 11 with zero re-tuning" has a loophole — it constrains only the MEASURE, while the per-observable structural template (rep/exponent/dimension) can absorb the fitting. And the existing lepton forms are heterogeneous (muon (24/π²)⁶ vs tau 49·71), so the sub-program's "one convention seeds all masses" premise presupposes a uniform template that does not yet exist.

*2026-06-14 Sunday 12:56 EDT.*. The team has adopted my downstream-blind test as the PLAN_TO_ALL primary success criterion. Applying the same discipline to it before ratification.

**The loophole in my own test.** "Freeze the convention from the substrate, then produce tau + quarks with zero re-tuning" constrains the *measure scale*. But every mass formula carries per-observable structure beyond the measure: the muon used (24/π²) where the exponent **6 = dim SO(4)** (a structural choice), the **Rac rep at ν=3/2** (a choice), **spatial-only vol(S⁴)** (a choice). If those per-mass structural assignments are selected per observable, the fitting simply *relocates* from the (now frozen) scale to the (still free) per-mass template. So "zero re-tuning" must cover the **whole template** — the scale AND the rules that fix which rep / which exponent / which volume-dimension each particle gets — derived from one uniform substrate algorithm fixed in advance. Freezing only the scale is necessary, not sufficient; it can pass while the real freedom hides one level down.

**The deeper issue this exposes about the sub-program's premise.** "Pin the boundary measure once → seeds all 12 masses" presupposes the masses share a common formula template differing only in the measure convention. They currently don't: the muon is **(24/π²)⁶** (sixth power of a volume ratio) and the tau is **49·71** (a product of Weyl-orbit integers). Those are structurally unrelated machinery. So either (a) there is a deeper uniform substrate template of which both are special cases — not yet found — or (b) each mass is its own bespoke construction, in which case freezing the measure buys far less than "seeds all masses" implies, and per-observable form-selection risk remains live for every one of them. **Step zero of the sub-program should therefore be establishing whether ONE substrate algorithm generates the heterogeneous-looking forms** — because until there is a single "the-rest"-generating procedure, the downstream-blind test can't even be evaluated (there is no uniform "rest" to predict, only twelve separate constructions).

**Net (no new fail; a sharpening):** my downstream-blind criterion, as I stated it, is too weak — it should read "freeze the full template (scale + per-observable assignment rules) from a uniform substrate algorithm, then predict the other 11 with zero re-tuning of *any* of it." And the sub-program's "solve once for all" framing should be held to the prerequisite that a uniform template exists; right now the lepton forms argue it doesn't yet. This makes the test harder and the premise more honest — which is the point. PLAN_TO_ALL amendment #3 should adopt the stronger wording. Posture unchanged: reactive on Lyra+Elie's independence sync and the derivations; count 2; muon IDENTIFIED.

## Cal #299 — VERIFIED Grace's Bergman-metric isotropy (flag (b) continuum side): independently confirmed analytically + symbolically; g_{ij̄} = 2·genus/(1−a²)² δ_ij at radial approach, anisotropic piece cancels exactly. Flag (b) CLOSED — credit Grace's math. Two precisions: (b) closes the volume VALUE not the measure CHOICE (so "24/π² justified at geometry level" overstates); and the (b) discrete cross-check WOULD be genuine over-determination (unlike (a)).

*2026-06-14 Sunday 16:23 EDT.*. My assigned verification ("Cal verifies cancellation pending"). Did it independently, not by trusting "seven figures."

**VERIFIED — Grace is right.** I worked the Wirtinger Hessian of log N (N = 1 − 2Σ|z_k|² + |Σz_k²|², the type-IV/Lie-ball norm) by hand and confirmed symbolically (sympy): at radial approach z = a·e₁, ∂_i∂_j̄ log N = −2/(1−a²)² δ_ij — g₁₁=g₂₂=g₃₃, all off-diagonals 0, **isotropic exactly.** Mechanism of the cancellation: ∂²N/∂z_i∂z̄_j = −2δ_ij + 4z_iz̄_j carries an anisotropic +4a²δ_{i1}δ_{j1} at the radial point, and the gradient term −(∂_iN)(∂_j̄N)/N² contributes exactly −4a²/(1−a²)²δ_{i1}δ_{j1}, which cancels it — a genuine feature of the holomorphic bilinear t=Σz_k², not an accident. So g_{ij̄} = 2·genus/(1−a²)² δ_ij (genus=5 → 10/(1−a²)²). **Credit to Grace: real computation, correct, independently reproduced.** This is the gate operating as builder, and it's the first positive math closure in the keystone work.

**What (b) closes:** the spatial S⁴ is round and unit-radius in the Bergman metric (conformal-to-flat at the boundary, no squishing) → the geometric volume is exactly vol(S⁴)=8π²/3, and Lyra's F127 "curvature operator = identity on the unit sphere" is justified, not assumed. My flag (b) is discharged on the continuum side.

**Precision 1 — what (b) does NOT close, and a Keeper overstatement to correct.** K362's table says "24/π² muon factor JUSTIFIED at geometry level." That overstates. (b) justifies the volume VALUE (8π²/3) *conditional on dividing by the geometric volume*. It does NOT justify the CHOICE to divide by geometric volume rather than normalize to the probability measure — and that choice is exactly what selects 24/π² over 64 (flag (a), the F59/ρ_commit measure-type question, still open, Lyra+Elie's lane). So: (b) removes the unit-radius sub-worry; the geometric-vs-probability decision that makes the muon land is untouched. The factor needs both the value (b ✓) and the choice (a, open). Don't let "(b) closed" read as "the muon factor is justified."

**Precision 2 — the (b) discrete cross-check is where over-determination can be REAL (unlike (a)).** Now that Grace closed R=1 on the continuum side (Bergman metric), Elie's GF(128) discrete cell-count → R=1 is a *genuinely different machinery* (discrete counting vs continuum differential geometry) converging on the same geometric fact — that survives my K360 one-coat catch, because it's not two vocabularies for one principle the way (a)'s heat-trace/discreteness both reduced to F59. So this cross-check is worth running (the independence caveat: the discrete derivation must not smuggle in the continuum metric). Useful steer: Elie's effort is better spent here (a real cross-check on (b)) than re-litigating (a) (where the "two routes" were one coat).

**On the bridge decision (Casey's PI call — referee input, not a ruling).** Lyra's framing is sound and her two conditions are the right bar: a single ontological bridge (mass=commitment-concentration), admissible only if (1) universal — one bridge for all 12 masses — and (2) prediction-validated (frozen convention carries 11 unknowns blind). The clarification that matters for the decision: **the PI ruling sets what "green" MEANS; it does not bank the muon.** Even if Casey rules "one universal prediction-validated bridge counts as zero-free-parameters" (Lyra's lean), the muon stays yellow until condition (2) is *delivered* — the ruling and the banking are separate events. And the bridge is candidate-not-earned: GR's "gravity=curvature" earned ontological status by predicting (Mercury, light-bending) *blind*; BST's bridge earns it the same way (predict the other 11 masses blind). Condition (2) IS my strengthened downstream-blind test (#298 — freeze the *full template*, not just the scale), and it's the decisive, currently-unmet one. So: legitimate kind of move, right conditions, not yet earned — and the ruling doesn't change the muon's tier today.

Count holds at 2; muon IDENTIFIED/yellow. (b) closed and verified; (a) open; the keystone awaits the measure-type answer + the blind prediction.

## Cal #300 — K365 (Grace's intensive/extensive correction + "spectrum check validates the bridge"): Grace's LABEL fix is correct and I own using the wrong label too. But the spectrum check 1:206.8:3477 does NOT validate ONE bridge — it uses THREE different formulas with TWO different volume-notions, which is the #298 uniform-template problem made concrete. "(a) closed" overstates: (a)-direction resolved (credit Grace), (a)-universality OPEN and now visibly so.

*2026-06-14 Sunday 17:55 EDT.*. Cold-read of K365. The chain caught the team's loose "extensive" framing (Grace); here's what the chain is getting loose about at K365.

**Credit + self-correction.** Grace's catch is right: the muon factor is 24/π² = (d_τ/d_μ) ÷ vol(S⁴) = content ÷ volume = a *concentration* = **intensive**, not content × volume = extensive. The "extensive" label that crept in (K359-K364) was wrong for single particles — and I propagated it too, in #297, when I called the shared principle "mass is extensive → un-normalized measure." Owning that: my Cal #35 *independence* point (the two routes are one principle) still stands, but I had the principle mislabeled; Grace's "intensive concentration" is the correct statement. And her reason for forbidding probability is genuinely better than the old one: the probability measure is **volume-blind** — it normalizes every stratum's spread to total mass 1, which erases the differing spread-volumes that ARE the mass differences, collapsing the spectrum. That's a clean forbid-not-permit (meets #296(c)). Real sharpening of flag (a)'s direction.

**The catch (Cal #27 at peak elegance — the #298 problem, now concrete).** K365 presents "spectrum check: 1 : 206.8 : 3477 = observed lepton ratios" as validating the corrected bridge, and celebrates "discrete-vs-continuum split shows up precisely where it should." But look at what the three numbers actually use:
- **electron** = α¹² tower (Elie's lead: m_e = 6π⁵·α¹²·m_Pl), or the reference 1;
- **muon** = continuum **(24/π²)⁶** — content ÷ **continuum** vol(S⁴)=8π²/3, raised to exponent 6 = dim SO(4);
- **tau** = discrete integer **49·71** — a Weyl-count ÷ a **point** (vol→1), exponent 1.

These are **three structurally different formulas**: different content-types (formal-degree ratio 64 vs Weyl-count 49·71 vs α-tower), different exponents (6 vs 1), and **two different notions of "volume"** (the muon's continuum Riemannian 8π²/3 — which Grace herself just verified, #299/#362 — vs the tau's integer cell-count). Note "concentration = content/volume" *literally diverges* at the tau (a point has volume → 0), so the tau cannot use the muon's formula at all; it's handed a separate discrete count. **So the spectrum check is not ONE bridge producing three masses — it's three constructions each hitting its target.** "Discrete-vs-continuum split shows up where it should" is celebrating the non-uniformity, not resolving it: continuum-for-muon and discrete-for-tau is form-selection *unless* the stratification forces which-is-which AND the three formulas are shown to be one rule evaluated at three strata. Neither is shown.

**Honest tier (correcting "two flags closed").** (b) is closed and verified (#299). (a) has TWO parts: **(a)-direction** — geometric/concentration over probability — is now well-argued (Grace's volume-blindness); call it resolved, credit. **(a)-universality** — one bridge, not three formulas — is OPEN, and K365's spectrum check made it *more* visibly open by laying the three distinct forms side by side. So "two of Cal's flags closed" overstates; it's "(b) closed + (a)-direction resolved + (a)-universality open and sharper."

**The α¹² lead deepens the problem.** Keeper rightly tiers it as α-tower-numerology-trap, not banked — good. But note: each new per-particle formula that hits its target makes "one universal bridge" *harder*, not easier. α¹² for the electron is a THIRD unrelated-looking form. Until the forms unify, every fresh per-particle hit is one more thing the single rule must later reproduce — accumulating obligations, not reductions.

**The concrete unification test (ties to Casey's "count commitments").** If it's all counting, the muon's *continuum* vol(S⁴)=8π²/3 must be re-expressible in the *same* volume-notion as the tau's *integer* cell count (1) — and the exponents (6 vs 1) and content-types (formal-degree vs Weyl-count) must all fall out of one rule applied at strata of different dimension. The honest obligation list for "one bridge": (i) one volume-notion covering point AND surface (continuum 8π²/3 ↔ integer count — currently different); (ii) one exponent rule (why 6 for muon, 1 for tau); (iii) one content-type (why formal-degree ratio here, Weyl-count there). Until those three reconcile, "concentration is mass" is the *muon's* bridge with the tau and electron on separate formulas — a principle, not yet a universal derivation, and the spectrum check is not its validation.

Count holds at 2; muon yellow. (b) verified; (a)-direction resolved (Grace, credit); (a)-universality is the live keystone and the three-formula spectrum check is where the team is at risk of reading agreement-with-targets as bridge-validation. The downstream-blind test on a SINGLE frozen rule (#298) remains the only thing that converts this to green.

## Cal #301 — BACKLOG COLD-READ (K273-K365): the load-bearing catch is K322's SO(10) claim vs the STANDING Five-Absence "No GUT / No proton decay" principle. Flat contradiction with the program's sharpest published falsifier, un-reconciled, in the cascade celebrated as "the most substantive advance." Plus a Cal #35 decomposition of "one integer → 5 structures." Secondary watch-items noted.

*2026-06-15 Monday 08:05 EDT.*. Independent verification pass over the K273-K365 arc (Monday assignment), via two read-only backlog maps + close read of the gauge cluster K321-K326 and the Five-Absence principle.

### THE CATCH — K322 (SO(10) "grand unification backbone from n_C=5") contradicts the STANDING Five-Absence falsifier.

**What K322 claims** (Lyra F103, "most substantive advance"): D_IV⁵ is complex-5-dim → tangent spin group Spin(2·n_C)=Spin(10); chiral spinor 16 = 2^(n_C−1); "SO(10) IS the spin group of the substrate's own shape... the whole backbone of **grand unification** — SO(10) and the 16 — falls out of ONE INTEGER n_C=5"; one generation = the 16; "SO(10)-16 COMPLETENESS" is the load-bearing mechanism for the β-functions. The word "grand unification / GUT backbone" is used throughout, without caveat.

**What the STANDING Five-Absence principle says** (Casey-named, Tuesday 2026-05-20; outreach-ready 1-pager): **"No GUT. SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1) is forced by D_IV⁵... no embedding into a larger simple Lie group required or available."** "Gauge groups from DISTINCT features... **Never unify.**" **"No proton decay (τ_p = ∞). Direct consequence of no GUT → no dim-6 operators → strict zero decay rate."** Refutation condition: **"proton decay (which would imply GUT)."**

**The contradiction is flat.** SO(10) is precisely "a larger simple Lie group" containing the SM groups, and K322 says it is not only *available* but IS the substrate's spin group — directly negating "no larger simple group available." And SO(10)+16 is the canonical proton-decay setup in standard physics (gauged SO(10) gives baryon-violating gauge bosons / dim-6 operators). So K322 puts the program's **sharpest, actively-tested falsifier** (proton decay = ∞; Hyper-K/DUNE looking now) at risk, and the Five-Absence justification for it ("no GUT → no dim-6 operators") is undermined the moment the SO(10)-16 structure is admitted. Nothing in K321-K326 mentions Five-Absence, NO-GUT, or proton decay — the team adopted full GUT language at peak elegance without checking the standing commitment. This is exactly Cal #27 (fires hardest at peak elegance) on a program-falsifier-level conflict.

**The reconciliation that's POSSIBLE but UN-DONE.** There is a coherent escape: SO(10) as a *classification* group (the 16 organizes one generation's fermions) that is **never gauged** — the three SM couplings live on distinct strata (K325), never unify, so no X/Y bosons, no coupling unification, no proton decay. SO(10)-classifying ≠ SO(10)-gauged. IF that's the intended reading, three things must happen before either claim is sound, let alone external:
  1. **K322 must scope SO(10) explicitly as classification-not-gauge** — drop "grand unification / GUT backbone," which states the opposite.
  2. **The Five-Absence brief must amend "no larger simple group available"** — by BST's own new result one IS available (the tangent Spin(10)); the honest wording is "available as a classification structure but never gauged → no unification."
  3. **"No proton decay" must be re-derived from the never-gauged / strata-separation mechanism, NOT from "no GUT"** — because the GUT group is now present as classification, a skeptic will say "you have the 16 and SO(10); show me specifically why the dim-6 baryon-violating operators are absent." That obligation is new and live.
Until all three, the program's biggest advance and its sharpest falsifier are inconsistent, and **neither can go external** (the Five-Absence 1-pager is outreach-ready; K322 is celebrated internally). This is the highest-priority reconciliation in the backlog.

### Cal #35 on K322's "one integer n_C=5 → 5+ structures" (Schur claim).
K322 bills "ONE integer forces Spin(10) + 16 + 3 generations + hypercharge formula + anomaly cancellation" as a Schur generator at peak. Decomposed, it is **one identification + standard rep theory**, not five independent forcings:
- n_C=5 → real-dim-10 → **Spin(10) tangent group** is *trivial* (any complex-5-fold has Spin(10) tangent structure — definitional, not a physics result).
- The single real, *unforced* move is **identifying that tangent Spin(10) with the SO(10) that classifies fermions.** That's the interesting leap — and it's an identification, not "forced by n_C=5."
- Given SO(10): the **16** (chiral spinor), the **hypercharge formula** Y/2 = T_3R+(B−L)/2 (standard SO(10)→SM embedding), and **anomaly cancellation** (the 16 is automatically anomaly-free) are *textbook consequences* — not independent substrate derivations.
- **3 generations** rides on the rank+1=3 strata count, which I flagged as itself unforced/degenerate (#286), not an independent result here.
So: one identification (substrate tangent Spin(10) = fermion SO(10)) wearing five hats, four of which are automatic once you adopt it. Credit the identification as a genuine, elegant move; but "one integer forces five things" overstates — n_C=5→Spin(10) is trivial, and the physics content is the single (unforced) identification plus standard rep theory. "Hypercharges DERIVED exactly" (K321) is RECOVERY of the standard SO(10) embedding, not a from-the-integers derivation — fine as recovery, but don't tier it "RIGOROUS + BANKED derivation."

### Credit — the self-correction discipline is active in this range too.
K324 walked back K323's "F63 firmed to Tier-0" (Grace tier correction: F105 is CANDIDATE). K327 (Elie withdrew "3/13") and K361 (three CIs withdrew an over-determination claim per a Cal catch) are clean walkbacks. So the team is self-correcting in the gauge arc — the SO(10)/Five-Absence conflict is the one that slipped through, precisely because it required checking a *new* claim against an *old standing* one (cross-temporal consistency), which the per-audit chain doesn't naturally do.

### Secondary watch-items (lower priority, Cal #35 territory, not yet load-bearing):
- **K325** "3 strata ↔ 3 masses ↔ 3 couplings" — a tidy 3↔3↔3↔3 (with generations); over-structuring flag, verify the three mappings are independent, not one stratification relabeled.
- **K349** "2^C₂=64 over-determined THREE WAYS (formal-degree / so(4) orientations / C₂ primary)" — check independence; 64=2⁶ and 6=C₂ is over-represented (same caveat as #300's three-hats / #286 small-coincident-integers).
- **K321** Schur "η_s=4s²−1/3 → 3 loop factors" — one formula → 3 values is fine as a *recovery* of the standard β-function spin coefficients; don't bill as substrate over-determination.
- **K326** "24 levers → ONE computation type; count climbs if projections forced" — count-motion language; the projections are not forced yet, so this is a *plan*, not a result. (Same discipline as the muon: forced-not-fit, then blind prediction.)

### Disposition.
Backlog pass complete; one program-level catch (K322 vs Five-Absence — reconcile before either goes external; re-derive no-proton-decay from never-gauged-strata, not from no-GUT), one Cal #35 deflation (one-integer→5 is one identification + rep theory), credit for the active walkbacks, four secondary watch-items. Count unaffected (the SO(10) content is "column (b)," not the 26 continuous — that framing is honest). Muon yellow, count 2.

## Cal #302 — Verified Grace's blind harness (the adjudicating instrument); credited F136 + Elie tau-box; affirmed cross-temporal sweep as standing methodology. Two calibration notes on the harness; independence/same-object tests on the deliverables.

*2026-06-15 Monday 08:15 EDT.*. Follow-on to the backlog catch (#301): verified the three K367 deliverables, ran the harness.

**Grace blind harness (play/grace_blind_harness.py) — VERIFIED, credit.** Ran it: muon fires 206.7612 vs 206.7683 = 0.00345% HIT from forced inputs only (64/vol(S⁴)=24/π², ^6=dim so(4)), zero fit. Tau slot genuinely empty in the real fire [A]; the candidate g³+2^C₂·g² appears only in a DEMO block explicitly marked "NOT a bank... must be DERIVED not asserted." Prints "count stays 2; scores, does not bank." This is the #298 downstream-blind test built right — pre-registered locked targets (2026-06-13), can't turn yellow→green. The instrument that will adjudicate the mass program is honest. Two calibration notes:
- **(a) The 1% floor is too loose for the demonstrated precision.** The muon hits at 0.003%; the candidate tau is 0.05% off (the known-missing −1.77 Weyl term, K345). At a 1% floor the candidate tau scores "HIT" *despite* a 0.05% unexplained gap — so "HIT" doesn't mean "as clean as the muon." Tighten the floor to the demonstrated precision (~0.01%, or base-rate-tied per Elie 4105) so a HIT means "as good as the muon," and keep the dev-print (which already exposes the 0.05% gap — good diagnostic). A binary HIT must not bless a miss that's hiding a missing term.
- **(b) The candidate tau form is role-ambiguous (verified by running it).** g^N_c+2^C₂·g^(N_c−1) (harness) = g^rank·(g+2^(g−1)) (Elie) = 3479 — *identical*, because N_c−1=rank and C₂=g−1. One form, not two competing ones (good) — but you cannot tell whether the exponent is "rank" or "N_c−1," or the 64 is "2^C₂" or "2^(g−1)": the integers coincide. So don't claim "the exponent is forced to be rank" when "N_c−1" fits identically; Elie's mechanism (side=g from Reed-Solomon) must specify which integer plays which role, or it inherits the #286 small-coincident-integer ambiguity.

**Lyra F136 (diagonal-vs-trace) — credit, right shape; directly resolves my #300 intensive/extensive.** Single-particle mass = diagonal heat kernel K(x,x,τ) (intensive, density per unit volume); Λ+gravity = trace ∫K dV (extensive). One operator H_B, two coefficient types — that's the clean account of why single-particle is intensive and bulk extensive, and it honestly notes F129 grabbed the trace when it should've been the diagonal. It also correctly relocates the remaining freedom to the #286 family-assignment (not the constant). Two tests it now owes: (i) the #300 universality test — does ONE diagonal-kernel expansion at the three strata GENERATE the three forms (α¹² / (24/π²)⁶ / 49·71), via e.g. a uniform (4πτ)^(−d/2)·Seeley-DeWitt exponent-from-stratum-dimension rule, or is it three evaluations relabeled? (ii) same-object check — is F136's "diagonal heat kernel" the SAME object as the harness's "(content/vol)^(dim so(4)) determinant over Λ²(TS⁴)"? If yes, unified; if two framings of the muon, that's a #300-multiplicity to resolve before claiming one bridge.

**Elie tau-box — credit the honesty (doesn't bank; names side=g open).** One independence flag on the "off-target item-2 link": the claim is "the SAME 64 forced in muon and tau." But the muon's 64 = d_τ/d_μ (Gindikin formal-degree ratio, F109) and the tau's 64 = 2^(g−1)=2^C₂ — both equal 2⁶, but from different origins (a polynomial ratio vs a power of 2). Same object (one mechanism appearing in both) or coincidence-of-64 (2⁶=2^C₂ is over-represented — #300/#349 caveat)? Verify the two 64s are structurally the same before calling it a cross-check; otherwise it's two roads to a small common number, not over-determination.

**Cross-temporal sweep as standing methodology — affirm (custodian).** Keeper named it: per-audit catches LOCAL discipline; a NEW claim contradicting an OLD standing principle (K322 vs Five-Absence) is only caught by sweeping the new claim against the named-principle register (Five-Absence, mass-as-concentration, three-strata, etc.). This is a genuine new methodology element — the audit chain is temporally local by construction; standing-principle consistency needs an explicit periodic cross-sweep. Affirm it as standing; credit Keeper for naming it. (It's the generalization of what #301 did by hand.)

Count 2, muon yellow. Harness honest and verified; F136 the right shape (owes the universality + same-object tests); tau-box honest (owes the 64-independence check + side=g); the SO(10)/Five-Absence reconciliation (#301) remains the load-bearing follow-up.

Not urgent-vs-data; urgent-vs-self-consistency.

Net: agree research forward is primary; agree parts 1&2 are parallel/external. Elevate part 3 from "external prep" to "internal derivation BST owes itself" — it's the same kind of obligation as the other forward work, not a presentation chore.

## Cal #305 — CROSS-TEMPORAL PASS (assigned): SECOND K322-vs-Five-Absence conflict found — F103's "16 = SM + ν_R" vs Five-Absence "No right-handed neutrino sector." Verbatim contradiction, un-reconciled, same SO(10)-16 root. [scope: INTERNAL-consistency + EXTERNAL brief; no count impact]

*2026-06-15 Monday 09:01 EDT.*. Ran the assigned cross-temporal sweep (new claims vs the standing named-principle register) for further K322-style conflicts. Found one — and it's tighter than the NO-GUT one.

**The conflict (verbatim).** F103 (the SO(10) "most substantive advance"): "the content... is 3×16, **the SM + ν_R**" — the SO(10) 16 contains, by standard rep theory, exactly 15 SM Weyl fermions **+ 1 right-handed neutrino**. Five-Absence 1-pager, entry "No sterile neutrinos": "**No right-handed neutrino sector** in the BST substrate's K-type Wallach decomposition." So F103 *adds* a right-handed-neutrino sector (the 16th component, "forced by n_C=5"); the standing external brief *denies* one. Direct contradiction. Grep confirms no recent note reconciles it. Same root as the NO-GUT conflict (#301): the SO(10)-16 adoption.

**Reconciliation — same pattern as the proton-decay fix (toy 4190).** Distinguish the *falsifiable claim* from the *stated mechanism*:
- KEEP (falsifiable, fine): no LIGHT sterile neutrino / no 3+1 oscillation signal (MicroBooNE/IceCube) / no 4th-generation neutrino. F103 actually *supports* the no-4th-gen half (exactly 3×16, no 4th).
- AMEND (now false): "no right-handed neutrino sector." By BST's own F103 there IS one — the 16's ν_R. Honest wording: "a *heavy* (seesaw) right-handed neutrino in the 16, NOT a light sterile state — no 3+1 oscillation signal." Same shape as "SO(10) available as classification, never gauged."

**Why it's INTERNAL, not just brief-wording — and the M_ν tie.** BST now needs M_ν (the 7-parameter neutrino blocker: 3 masses + PMNS). The natural mechanism with ν_R in the 16 is the **seesaw** — which *commits* BST to the heavy ν_R. So the M_ν derivation (Grace's next big piece) implicitly uses exactly the right-handed-neutrino sector the brief denies. That makes this a live internal-consistency question, not a wording tweak: is BST's M_ν a seesaw using the 16's ν_R (→ heavy RH neutrino, consistent with no-light-sterile) or not? The answer constrains the M_ν derivation AND must be stated before the Five-Absence brief goes external. BST currently holds three commitments — "no RH sector" (brief), "16 = SM+ν_R" (F103), and "needs M_ν" (likely seesaw) — and the first contradicts the other two.

**Pass verdict + recommendation.** The recent SO(10)-16 arc generates TWO Five-Absence conflicts (NO-GUT/proton-decay #301; NO-sterile/RH-neutrino #305), both from one root, both fixable by the same classification-not-gauge / heavy-not-light distinction. Recommend a SINGLE consolidated "SO(10)-16 reconciliation" doc covering proton-decay AND the RH-neutrino, since they're one structural source — rather than patching the brief twice. Otherwise the arc is clean against the standing register: checked mass-as-concentration (F136 consistent), three-strata (consistent), no-4th-generation (F103 supports it), Casey #14 signature (untouched). This is the cross-temporal sweep working as the standing discipline Keeper named — and it's the generalization of #301: the per-audit chain is temporally local; new claims must be swept against the named-principle register, and the SO(10) adoption tripped two standing entries, not one.

## Cal #306 — K325 "project don't unify" reframe: good strategy (Five-Absence-consistent; reinforces the #301 reconciliation; α-as-projection proof-of-concept) wrapped in two over-claims. AND the key integration: K322 and K325 are internally in tension — mutually consistent ONLY under the #301 reconciliation, so that reconciliation is INTERNALLY load-bearing, not just external. [scope: CLAIM-tier framing + INTERNAL-consistency; no count impact]

*2026-06-15 Monday 09:04 EDT.*. Last high-value backlog-sweep item; ties the gauge arc together.

**Credit — the reframe is a legitimate strategy and Five-Absence-consistent.** Elie's honest negative is real (the three couplings cross 4 orders apart; naive SO(10) sin²θ_W=0.207 vs 0.231, 10% miss) — but it is NOT a failure for BST, because the standing Five-Absence principle already PREDICTS no coupling unification ("the 10¹⁶ GeV convergence is RG-kinematic, not physical"). So Casey's "project don't unify, each coupling at its own stratum" RE-AFFIRMS the standing position, and α=1/137 (forced from N_max, never run to a GUT scale) is a genuine proof-of-concept that BST reads couplings off structure, not RG-convergence. Good move, correctly tiered (count stays 2; values pending).

**Key integration — K322 and K325 are internally in tension, and the #301 reconciliation is what resolves it.** K322 says "the whole backbone of **grand unification** — SO(10) — falls out"; K325 says "**don't unify** — three strata, no GUT." Those two recent claims pull opposite directions on unification. They are mutually consistent ONLY under the #301 reading: SO(10) is a **classification** group (K322's 16) AND the gauge couplings are **three-strata-projected, never gauged-unified** (K325). So the SO(10)-as-classification reconciliation is not merely required-before-external (#301) — it is **internally load-bearing**: without it, two of the program's own recent advances (K322, K325) contradict each other. This is a second internal reason (joining the proton-decay one, #304a) that the reconciliation belongs in the research queue, not the external-prep bucket — it's what makes the gauge arc self-consistent. [strengthens #303: INTERNAL]

**Catch 1 [CLAIM-tier] — the same rank+1=3 over-extended to two independent triples.** K325: "the same r+1=3 stratification that generates 3 lepton generations also generates 3 gauge coupling scales." But 3 fermion *generations* and 3 *gauge groups* are physically INDEPENDENT triples (no law ties #generations to #gauge-groups); the SM having both = 3 is a coincidence, not a structural identity. Forcing both onto the same 3 strata is the #286 coincidence-of-3 doing double duty. And the gauge→stratum mapping is admittedly "pending/candidate" — 3!=6 assignment freedom — with internal mismatches: SU(3) is sourced from N_c=3 but assigned to the *dim-n_C=5* bulk stratum (3≠5), and the assignment puts the **colorless electron and the color group SU(3) on the same (bulk) stratum**, which is physically backwards (the electron is an SU(3) singlet). So "3 couplings forced at 3 strata" is a HOPE, not forced; the assignment is an unforced 6-way form-selection with mismatches.

**Catch 2 [CLAIM-tier] — "unification wall dissolved" overstates; it's relocated, not dissolved.** The wall is *computing the right coupling values* (e.g. sin²θ_W = 0.231). "Project don't unify" doesn't compute them — it RELOCATES the obligation from "make the three meet" to "project each from its own stratum," and that projection is **uncomputed** (K325 lists α_s and sin²θ_W values as "pending stratum identification + projection"). So the 10%-off sin²θ_W is still 10% off until a stratum projection is shown to give 0.231. Honest framing: the wall is *relocated and unverified*, not dissolved. (K325 admits "values pending" — good — but the "wrong framing / dissolved" language oversells; the same discipline as α=1/137 being a real anchor vs the rest being uncomputed.)

**Tier.** K325 is honest on the count (stays 2; reframe banks no value). The over-claims are in the "dissolved / forced / generates both" FRAMING, not the count. Disposition: credit the strategy and the Five-Absence-consistency; bank "project-not-unify" as a candidate program-direction (α proof-of-concept real); do NOT bank "3 couplings forced at 3 strata" (unforced assignment + mismatches) or "wall dissolved" (relocated, uncomputed); and note the SO(10)-classification reconciliation is internally load-bearing (makes K322+K325 consistent).

**Sweep status:** the gauge cluster (K321-326), the muon cascade (K348-365, = #296-300), and the keystone three-strata are now cold-read; cross-temporal pass done (two Five-Absence conflicts, #301+#305). Remaining secondary watch-items (K280 two-lanes-156.4MeV convergence; K286 three-lever 2→13 target) are lower-value; I'm reactive on the cell-map / harness / M_ν as they land. Count 2, muon yellow.

## Cal #307 — K372 cascade: CREDIT the genuine exponent-6 mechanism (real geometric derivation + falsifiable n_C-dependence), but "6 = ONE OBJECT FOUR FACES" is a degenerate STAR — the SAME over-count as #304b ("64 three faces"), now twice in two cascades. PATTERN FLAG: apply Grace's own C3 (loops-not-stars) at generation-time. Plus: "lepton triangle one observable-loop from banking" overstates (the object-64 "loop" is that same degenerate star). [scope: CLAIM-tier framing; no count impact]

*2026-06-15 Monday 09:18 EDT.*. Cold-read of the K372 cascade.

**(1a) CREDIT — the exponent-6 derivation is genuine and falsifiable.** The mechanism: the muon (scalar) couples to boundary curvature; curvature is a 2-form in Λ²(T S⁴) = so(4); the norm factorizes over the six so(4) generators → determinant of six equal eigenvalues → power 6 = number of independent 2-planes (ways the boundary can curve). That is a real *geometric* derivation of the exponent (it counts curvature components), honestly tiered with the one rigor bolt flagged (prove the so(4)-determinant factorization is the correct tensor structure). And the falsifiable n_C-dependence is genuine: exponent = dim so(n_C−1) = C(n_C−1,2), so n_C=5→6, n_C=4→3, n_C=6→10 — only n_C=5 gives 6. That's a real forbidding structure. This is ONE forced object (the exponent via curvature-count), and it's good work.

**(1b) CATCH — "6 = ONE OBJECT, FOUR FACES" is a degenerate star, not over-determination, and it's the second cascade running.** The claimed four faces — 6 = exponent = dim so(4) = C₂ = log(boundary depth) — collapse to ONE: the exponent IS dim so(4) (the single identification just credited); dim so(4) = C₂ = 6 is the C₂-triple coincidence (#304b/#306, 6 over-represented); and "log of boundary depth" = log₂(64) = 6 is just the **64 restated** (the 64 that is itself the #304b over-counted object). So it is the number 6 named four ways — by Grace's OWN C3 ("TRUE over-determination is LOOPED not STARRED"), a STAR with four coincident spokes. It adds **zero** confirmation to the exponent derivation. This is the identical over-count to #304b ("64 = three faces") one cascade later. **Pattern:** the team keeps minting "one object, N faces" as over-determination, and they keep being degenerate stars (coincident via the small-integer facts C₂=dim so(4)=6, 64=2⁶). Grace's C3 is the corrective — but it has to fire at *generation* time, on the "N faces" claim itself, not after. Recommend: stop banking "one object N faces" as over-determination; bank the ONE genuine derivation (here: exponent = curvature-component count, modulo the rigor bolt); note the other "faces" are the same number appearing elsewhere, which is not confirmation.

**(2) "Lepton triangle ONE observable-loop from banking the sector" — overstates [CLAIM-tier].** Elie 4194 does good discipline (distinguishing an OBJECT-level loop from an OBSERVABLE-level loop; recognizing a true observable-loop needs an independent tau↔muon step = the unproven one-trajectory structure, K342; unifying Grace's C3 with Lyra's one-vs-three-trajectories — credit). BUT the claimed "genuine loop on object 64 (three independent derivations)" inherits #304b: the three derivations of 64 are NOT independent (two collapse via C₂=dim so(4)), so the object-64 "loop" is the same degenerate star — it's not a loop. So the honest state is: there is **no genuine loop yet** — the object-64 "loop" is a star, and the observable-loop requires the *unproven* one-trajectory structure. So "one observable-loop closure away from banking" → "needs the one-trajectory structure proven (K342, open) AND the object-64 'loop' re-examined (it's a degenerate star, not three independent paths)." Not one step away; two unproven things away.

**(3) "One investigation, four threads" (commitment-to-cell map) — genuine convergence, but it RAISES the forced-ness bar [WATCH].** The cell-map gating tau-box + M_ν (7 params) + commitment-process + observable-loop is real convergence (solve-once, good). Per #294: "one work unblocks N things" is a criticality statement, not N reductions, and the count-impact is bounded by the inputs the cell-map *forces*. The more it gates, the *higher* the bar on its forced-ness — if the cell-map has internal freedom, that freedom propagates to all four threads (and to 7 params via M_ν). So credit the convergence; flag that it raises, not lowers, the verification bar — leverage is real only if the object is forced (the M_ν forced-vs-free check, #305, is the load-bearing one).

**(4) Lyra ν=1/2 M_ν lead — honest (credit), and integrate with #305.** The ν=1/2 unoccupied ρ-slot as a neutrino-stratum candidate is honestly tiered (lead not derivation; blind to neutrino masses; flags 3-neutrinos-vs-1-slot as the real work — good). Integration note: ν=1/2 reads as the LIGHT active-neutrino side (sub-unitarity, tiny mass), while #305's reconciliation needs the HEAVY ν_R from the 16 (seesaw). These are different objects; M_ν should be the **seesaw structure** (heavy ν_R in the 16 + light neutrinos at ν=1/2). Building it that way simultaneously (a) gives M_ν and (b) resolves the #305 no-sterile conflict (heavy ν_R, not light sterile). So the ν=1/2 lead and the #305 ν_R are the two ends of one seesaw — worth building together.

**(5) Lyra F131 star/loop retroactive understanding — credit, no catch.** Recognizing her withdrawn "two-route over-determination" was a STAR (one F59 source, two observables), consistent with #297; "grow stars into loops" is the right goal. Discipline maturing.

Disposition: muon geometry's genuine pieces are real (64 + 8π²/3 + exponent-6-via-curvature, each modulo its rigor bolt); the "one object N faces" over-determination framing is a recurring degenerate-star inflation (now ×2) that should stop being banked; "lepton sector one loop from banking" overstates (no genuine loop yet). Count honestly 2; muon yellow. I remain reactive on the cell-map / M_ν (forced-vs-free is the decisive check).

## Cal #308 — Casey's commitment-process picture (PI interpretive input): own + sharpen the "next-integer must be FORCED" watch-item (Keeper filed it as "Cal #374"; formally #308). The picture is coherent with two standing foundations (π-tile, SWPP) — credit; its one NEW load-bearing element (next-integer pre-selection) is exactly where a hidden parameter can hide, and it needs TWO forced-ness checks. The four-candidate parallel build is right; one Cal #35 note (they're ~3 distinct, not 4). [scope: INTERNAL forced-vs-chosen; no count impact]

*2026-06-15 Monday 09:31 EDT.*. Cold-read of Casey's picture (circle-tiles-sphere; absorb→commit→emit at the orbit; deposit at a point; pre-selected by the next integer) + Keeper's K373 routing.

**Numbering hygiene:** Keeper filed this as "Cal #374 candidate." My referee log is at #307; the next is #308. Formally entering it as **Cal #308** (the "374" doesn't match my sequence — same gentle correction as the Grace-#36 attribution; tag scope, use my numbers).

**Credit — the picture is coherent with established foundations, not ad hoc.** Two of its four elements re-ground STANDING pieces at the lepton level: "the circle that tiles the sphere" = **π as the substrate tile primitive** (OneGeometry foundational: circles tiling a sphere — the picture is consistent with the substrate's own tile primitive); "absorb → commit → emit" = the **SWPP** (Casey-named standing principle: substrate as absorption→commitment→emission cycle). So the picture isn't a new free construction — it re-expresses π-tile + SWPP as a lepton-scale deposition cycle, and ties to the K365 concentration=mass bridge (deposit at a point = concentration). Coherent. That coherence is a point in its favor.

**The one NEW load-bearing element, and the watch-item (own + sharpen).** The genuinely new input is **"the deposit point is pre-selected by the next integer"** — the determinism mechanism. This is exactly where a hidden free parameter can live (Cal precision (c): forbid the alternative; Cal #36/Grace blind-to-target). For the picture to survive discipline, "next integer" must be substrate-FORCED — and that splits into **two** checks, both required:
  (i) **The sequence is forced** — "next integer" is the next in a *uniquely-forced* substrate sequence (the primary chain rank→N_c→n_C→C₂→g→N_max, or the Frobenius powers x^(2^k) in GF(128), or the Reed-Solomon ladder) — NOT a choice among those sequences. If you get to pick which sequence, "next integer" is a chosen index.
  (ii) **The starting point / index is forced** — no freedom in where the sequence starts or which term a given particle gets.
If both are forced, the picture is genuinely powerful: deterministic deposition with NO free parameter (the "pre-selected" is substrate-forced, not fit-chosen). If EITHER is free, "pre-selected by the next integer" is "pre-selected by a parameter we chose," and the determinism is a fit wearing a mechanism's clothes. That is the verifiable question the team must answer as they build — and Keeper's investigation questions ("next in what sequence?") are exactly it.

**The forced-ness here is UPSTREAM of the M_ν forced-vs-free check (#305/#307).** This is the integration that matters: the commitment-process picture, IF forced, is what makes the commitment-to-cell map forced, which is what makes M_ν forced (not 7-free-knobs, #294/#305). So #308 (next-integer forced) and the M_ν forced-vs-free check are the *same forced-ness question at different levels* — the whole convergent investigation's validity rests on the next-integer being substrate-forced. That's the load-bearing thread to hold.

**The four-candidate parallel build — right discipline, one Cal #35 note.** Building all candidate interpretations in parallel without premature commitment (Casey selects on return) is exactly right. But check genuine distinctness: Casey's picture is "closest to Elie's (c) Frobenius-orbit-across-both, enriched" — so Casey's ≈ Elie's (c), and the four candidates are really ~3 distinct pictures (Elie (a) bulk-cell, (b) Shilov-boundary, (c)≈Casey-enriched), not 4 independent. Don't over-count the exploration; and when they're built, check whether (a)/(b)/(c) actually make *different* predictions for the cell-map (if they converge to the same map, the "four candidates" collapse, same as the heat-trace/discreteness one-principle case, #297).

Disposition: credit the picture's coherence (π-tile + SWPP + concentration-bridge); the decisive verifiable question is the next-integer forced-ness (two checks: sequence forced + index forced); it's the same forced-ness that gates M_ν's 7 params; the parallel build is right but it's ~3 distinct candidates. Count 2, muon yellow.

## Cal #313 — Cold-read of the two new #418 items: (1) the Wolfenstein "STANDS forward" residual = |ρ−iη| is parametrization ALGEBRA (definitional), not a derivation; (2) Grace's M splits into M_angle (3 angles) + M_phase (δ_CP), and the phase is currently NOT forced → even M_angle=0 gives max 3 forced CKM angles, not 4.
*2026-06-17 Wed 11:23 EDT.* Two reads, both refining Grace's decisive M-question.

**(1) The Wolfenstein result — credit the hierarchy STRUCTURE, but "residual = |ρ−iη| exactly" is definitional, not derived.** In the Wolfenstein parametrization V_ub = Aλ³(ρ−iη), V_us = λ, V_cb = Aλ², so V_us·V_cb = Aλ³ and |V_ub|/(|V_us||V_cb|) ≡ |ρ−iη| **identically** — it is true by the definition of the parametrization, for ANY values. So "residual 0.413 = |ρ−iη| = what should be there per Jarlskog, forward-verified" is parametrization ALGEBRA, not a forward derivation and not an independent confirmation. And the numbers don't match without it (V_us·V_cb = 0.00925 vs |V_ub| = 0.00382), so the residual 0.413 carries the discrepancy — and 0.413 is the OBSERVED CP magnitude, not derived. What IS creditable (structural-grade, verify): that su(3) composite-root structure FORCES V_ub to be the *composite* of the V_us and V_cb roots → the λ³ = λ·λ² *hierarchy structure*. Credit the hierarchy structure if Elie's derivation forces it; do NOT bank the residual/CP-magnitude as derived.

**(2) Grace's M splits — and the split changes the forecast.** CKM's 4 params = 3 real angles + 1 CP phase (δ_CP). The up/down *seat misalignment* is a real rotation → it can force the 3 ANGLES (M_angle). The CP PHASE needs a *complex* phase in the seat structure — a separate freedom (M_phase). Per (1), δ_CP is currently the *observed Wolfenstein residual*, i.e. NOT forward-derived → M_phase ≥ 1 right now. So even if the misalignment is fully forced (M_angle = 0), the clean motion is at most **3 angles, not 4** — δ_CP is separately gated until the complex-phase structure is forced. So Grace's M-question should be asked TWICE: is the *angle* misalignment forced (M_angle), AND is the *phase* forced (M_phase)? "4 CKM from M=0" → "3 angles if misalignment forced; δ_CP separately gated (currently observed)."

Credit: Keeper 15th (F177 = color half only; CKM lives in weak-isospin T_3^R misalignment, not color) + 16th (absorbed #312) self-corrections clean; convergence to one number M is healthy; Grace's R=4/M is the right instrument — it just needs the angle/phase split. Count publish 3 / internal 4 unchanged; CKM hierarchy structure earns structural-grade IFF the composite is forward-verified (not the CP residual).

So the two readings pull against each other; the cleaner reading is F(4) sl(2) = su(2)_R (R-symmetry), SM SU(2)_L in SO(5).

**VERDICT:** standard convention + construction = su(2)_R (R-symmetry); literature weight is decisively there. su(2)_L is a strong NON-standard reinterpretation that the branching does not establish — it needs the su(2) shown to GAUGE matter (not rotate supercharges). **Net:** keep the Coleman-Mandula insight — it's real and it strengthens the hinge — but restate it precisely (F(4) licenses BST's spacetime-internal *combination*, not "the chiral weak force," which the SM has without SUSY), and carry the no-superpartners obligation alongside it. - The **Cartan per-domain elimination is the RIGHT approach** — credit Casey. - **The load-bearing check (Cal #35):** the Strong-Uniqueness null-model (≈1.7×10⁻⁵, 10–11 legs) and "passing all five criteria simultaneously" rest on the legs/criteria being **independent**. Given this week's recurring degenerate-star pattern (N-fold claims collapsing to fewer independent), the independence of the five substrate criteria / the 10–11 legs MUST be verified before the significance is banked — if several legs derive from the same primary, the null-model overstates and the uniqueness is softer than claimed. **Publication-boundary discipline (publisher lane, Calibration #19):**
1. **Explicit disclaimer in Paper A:** "This is NOT a solution to the Clay Yang–Mills problem as stated (all compact simple G). **Net:** approve the strategy — it's honest and it's the right move. Support it by (i) making Paper B airtight on independence-of-criteria (the foundation), and (ii) holding the publication boundary (disclaimer + conjecture-S + conditional-gap). **The cross-channel match's genuine test is the NON-0⁺⁺ channels.** The 0⁺⁺ anchor is *inside* the set being matched — it's where W_p(2-form)=+1 came from — so reproducing it is necessary-not-sufficient (circular for that channel). The blind predictions are the OTHER J^PC channels (1⁺⁻ adjoint, 2⁺⁺ traceless-14, etc.), where W_p is NOT pre-constrained. Score THOSE. And the discipline (Grace's criterion, correct): W_p must be ONE curvature computation per form-degree, dropped into all channels with NO per-channel knobs — if W_p is adjusted per channel to match, it's fitted (the per-channel-knob the criterion forbids). So the honest cross-channel verdict, when Elie runs it: blind on the non-anchor channels, W_p single-per-degree-from-curvature, all channels simultaneously, no convention rescue of any miss.

## Cal #334 — Sharpening #332 for the pending c-function computation (Lyra absorbed #332 cleanly, found a sharper tell): the decisive check-1 discriminator is to derive the trace-formula seminorm count as a FUNCTION OF n and evaluate where rank ≠ ⌊n/2⌋ (e.g., n=3: rank=2 vs ⌊n/2⌋=1; n=7: rank=2 vs ⌊n/2⌋=3). Constant (=rank=2) → innocent; n-dependent (=⌊n/2⌋) → dimension smuggled. Check-3 structural form: N_c=m_s is a derivation IFF SU(3)_color acts on the m_s=3 short-root-multiplicity space (color = symmetry of the 3 copies), not "two 3's that match." Even if check 1 resolves, checks 2+3 remain — check-1-resolving ≠ innocence-restored. Count holds 4.
*2026-06-21 Sun 11:27 EDT.* Lyra absorbed #332 cleanly and articulated the tell more sharply than I did ("rank=2 and ⌊n_C/2⌋=2 agree exactly at the answer and nowhere else — the fingerprint of a quantity secretly reading off the conclusion"). That generalizes into a reusable anti-circularity diagnostic: *when two candidate justifications for a quantity agree only at the target value, you cannot distinguish them at the target — compute away from it.* Worth keeping as a standing test (same shape as the forced-vs-fitted and degenerate-star checks).

**The concrete discriminator for check 1 (the gift to Grace's c-function lane):** don't evaluate at n=5 (where rank and ⌊n/2⌋ coincide). Derive the trace-formula **seminorm count as a function of n**, then evaluate where rank ≠ ⌊n/2⌋ — type IV_n is rank 2 for ALL n, but ⌊n/2⌋ = 2 only at n ∈ {4,5}. So at n=3 (rank 2, ⌊n/2⌋=1) or n=7 (rank 2, ⌊n/2⌋=3) the two readings *diverge*. If the convergence requires rank-many (2, constant) seminorms → the bound is dimension-free → innocence survives. If it requires ⌊n/2⌋ (n-dependent) → the dimension is smuggled → R3 names the dimension. n=7 is the cleaner test case (m_s=5 ≥ 3, so the limit genuinely converges there; n=3 fails m_s≥3 outright). That's a real Harish-Chandra/Plancherel computation, and Lyra was right to route it to Grace rather than assert it.

**Check-3 form (so the team knows what to establish):** N_c = m_s = 3 is a *derivation* (not a #286 coincidence) IFF the SU(3)_color acts on the m_s=3 short-root-multiplicity space — i.e., color is the symmetry group of the three short-root copies (color FROM the short roots). That ties to the #418 color-from-strata work (8 = 2 Cartan + 6 roots su(3)); the check is whether *that* SU(3) is the one acting on the m_s=3 multiplicity space. Plausible and checkable; "two 3's that match" is the failure mode.

**One caution:** even if check 1 resolves (the bound is rank-driven), that does NOT restore full innocence — checks 2 (R5 independence/prior-motivation) and 3 (N_c=m_s structural) are equally load-bearing. Lyra has this right (v0.3 keeps "maximal innocence" out until all three close). Don't let check-1-resolving read as "innocence restored."

**On Casey's routing question (draft v0.3 now vs wait for Grace):** referee view — draft v0.3 NOW with the condition stated explicitly ("sharpened toward innocence; airtightness pending checks 1–3, decisive = the c-function seminorm-count away from n=5"). ## Cal #335 — Catch on the "two independent integer-3 identifications = substrate-Schur-generator territory" framing: it's BACKWARDS. *2026-06-21 Sun 11:45 EDT.* Credit-heavy with one framing catch.

**CREDIT — the discipline is exemplary:** Grace's "corpus-check before computation" caught her own B₂/C₂ mislabel before it touched the exponent computation (and confirmed nothing load-bearing depended on it) — a genuine pre-computation discipline. Lyra's J-parity obstruction is a real structural finding: the short-root space is 3 real (odd) directions, J needs even real dimension, so J cannot act as a complex structure on it — the *obvious* "color-from-short-roots" route is blocked. My #332 Check 3 ("N_c=m_s derivation or 3=3 coincidence?") is vindicated — "N_c=3 SOLID color" walked back, correctly. Lyra tiered it honestly ("lead with caveat, not result"). 10 honest events, none manufactured.

**THE CATCH — "two independent integer-3 identifications = substrate-Schur-generator territory" is exactly backwards.** A substrate-Schur generator (Casey-Schur discipline / Cal #35) is ONE substrate property forcing MULTIPLE observables — *common source, multiple readings*. Here the natural common source (J promoting the short-root multiplicity to SU(3) color) is OBSTRUCTED by parity, and the surviving color derivation (h^∨(SU(3))=3, Elie engine) is *independent of the short-root geometry*. So m_s=3 (root multiplicity) and h^∨=3 (color) are TWO SEPARATE facts that both equal 3, with **no demonstrated common source** — and the obstruction is *positive evidence against* one. That is the #286 coincidence-of-small-integers, **the opposite of a Schur generator.** A Schur generator would *resolve* two-3's-that-match by exhibiting the common source; the J-obstruction shows the obvious common source fails. So: tier it COINCIDENCE (two 3's, common source obstructed), not Schur/over-determination. Don't let "independent" read as "strength" — for a *common-source* claim, independence-plus-obstruction is weakness.

**Consequence for Paper B (this is the load-bearing part):** the v0.2/v0.4 centerpiece — "the same invariant m_s=3 gives BOTH dimension AND color" — is now **half-true.** m_s forces the **dimension** (dim_C = rank + m_s = 5, solid). m_s does **NOT** force the **color**: the m_s→SU(3) link is parity-obstructed, and N_c=3 comes from h^∨, a *separate* 3. So Check 3 isn't merely "a pending lead" — the J-obstruction is positive evidence the obvious link fails, so **"color from m_s" should come OUT of the criteria-innocence spine** (retained only as a pending non-obvious-workaround lead, route i). The spine's honest claim downgrades to: **"(rank, m_s) forces the DIMENSION uniquely; the color N_c=3 is a separate identification (h^∨), not read off the same invariant."** Domain-uniqueness (dim=5) intact and real; "color also falls out of the same root invariant" broken. The abstract's "(rank, m_s) forces dim AND color, neither in the criteria" must drop the "AND color" claim to "dim; color separately."

**Net:** the domain uniqueness via (rank, m_s)→dim=5 is solid (credit). The color-from-m_s leg is broken by the parity obstruction (not just unproven). N_c=3 is a separate 3 — coincidence with m_s=3 until a common source is found, NOT a Schur generator. Paper B forces the dimension; color is a separate fact. Count HOLDS 4.

## Cal #338 — BACKSTOP on "W1 FOLDED in 20 minutes": W1 was REDUCED to W2 (exactly my #337 reduction), NOT closed. What closed in 20 min are the EASY parts — the OS-axiom INGREDIENTS for the generalized-free bulk structure (Grace) + the su(3) gauge ALGEBRA via bilinear Schwinger (Elie 4301, real, credit). What did NOT close is W1's hard part: the existence of the INTERACTING 4D YM QFT (OS for the full interacting Schwinger functions), which = W2-completeness = "THE one remaining question" (Keeper's own map). Three conflations: (i) OS-for-generalized-free ≠ OS-for-interacting; (ii) non-abelian su(3) ALGEBRA ≠ interacting QFT EXISTS; (iii) #418-closed (algebra) ≠ W1-closed (QFT existence). #337 CONFIRMED not refuted: a/b are BST-internal (fast, Casey right); c is open-in-math (= W2, open). Methodology candidate amended: engage+build for BST-internal, but "open-in-math" is NOT always a non-engagement label. Count holds 4.
*2026-06-21 Sun 13:58 EDT.*

**CREDIT the genuine 20-minute work — Casey's "20 minutes" is vindicated for the BST-internal parts:**
- Elie 4301 (6/6): bilinear Schwinger operators realize su(3) (CCR + 81 gl(3) commutators + A₂ relations close). Real, clean, and it resolves his linear-vs-bilinear fork in favor of bilinear. The gauge ALGEBRA is su(3), verified. Genuinely ~20 minutes — Casey right that this was BST-internal, not multi-week.
- Grace: the bulk D_IV⁵ has the OS-axiom *ingredients* (H², Casimir-gap, Szegő-positive, SO(4,2)). Real.
- Lyra caught the non-commutative-Toeplitz ≠ interaction shortcut (free fields also have non-commutative operator algebras) before banking. Good discipline.

**But "W1 FOLDED / the wall map collapsed" OVERSTATES — and it's the exact #337 conflation materialized.** The three pieces close the EASY parts, not the hard one:
- **(i) OS-for-generalized-free ≠ OS-for-interacting.** OS axioms are about the *Schwinger functions* (n-point correlators) of a *field theory*. A reproducing-kernel Hilbert space + Casimir Hamiltonian is a representation-theoretic / one-particle object; reflection positivity of the *Szegő kernel* (a two-point reproducing kernel) is the generalized-free two-point structure, NOT reflection positivity of the full *interacting* Schwinger functions. Grace *herself* flagged earlier: "exact transfer gives generalized-free, not interacting YM." So the bulk OS-ingredients are the easy part (= #337 a/b), not the interacting QFT.
- **(ii) non-abelian su(3) ALGEBRA ≠ interacting QFT EXISTS.** Lyra's "interacting ⟺ non-abelian su(3) ⟺ #418": the non-abelian algebra gives the *classical* interaction vertex (A∧A), but constructing the interacting *quantum* theory (interacting Schwinger functions existing + satisfying OS) is the open constructive problem — that's *why* Clay is hard; everyone knows the gauge group is non-abelian. Having su(3) is the classical input, not the QFT existence. This is the *same* error class as the Toeplitz shortcut Lyra caught, one level up (algebra structure ≠ QFT existence).
- **(iii) #418-closed ≠ W1-closed.** Elie's su(3) realization closes the *algebra*; W1 is the *QFT existence*. Different objects.

**So what actually happened: W1's easy parts closed (OS-ingredients + su(3)-algebra, fast — Casey right), and W1's HARD part (interacting 4D YM QFT existence) was RELABELED W2 — "THE one remaining question," still open (Keeper's own map says so).** That is *exactly my #337 reduction* (W1 → a/b done + c = interacting = W2, open) — **confirmed, not refuted.** "Exactly as you predicted [closeable]" inverts it: #337 predicted a/b tractable + c open-in-math; that's what occurred. Nothing open-in-math got closed; the open-in-math part is now called W2.

**The methodology candidate needs amending.** "Engage, don't label; build the concrete model" is RIGHT for BST-*internal* computations (Elie's su(3) proved it — that "multi-week" was over-caution). It is DANGEROUS as stated for the other class: "open in math itself" is NOT always "an admission of non-engagement" — for W1-the-interacting-QFT-existence it's a *true statement about a problem the field hasn't solved in 50 years*, independent of how hard BST engages. So amend: **"engage and build the concrete model — AND distinguish BST-internal computations (where 'multi-week' is over-caution) from open-in-math problems (where 'open' is a true statement about the field, not a non-engagement label)."** The discipline against under-engagement must NOT become a license to relabel open-math problems as closed-by-engaging. That relabeling is precisely what "W1 folded" does.

**Paper A consequence (#331, load-bearing):** Paper A must say **Δ = C₂ = 6 GIVEN the duality; W1 reduced to W2 (the interacting-QFT/completeness question); W2 = the genuine open core.** NOT "W1 folded / YM essentially done." The whole Be-Polite strategy's honesty (#331) rests on W1/W2 staying honestly open — "we explained physical YM conditionally," not "we solved it." If Paper A ships with "W1 folded," that's the one claim a referee dismantles on sight, and it's the false claim the strategy was built to avoid. Count HOLDS 4.

**Disposition:** Paper A v0.1 PASSES the spine; ship-blocked on TRIM 1 (the F268 absorption — load-bearing); TRIMs 2–3 before external. The honest two-condition-conditional architecture is correct and should not be weakened. — Cal
